data.HEADLINE: The Post-Workout Protein Window
data.BODY: Your muscles are most receptive to protein within 30-60 minutes after training. Prioritize a fast-digesting source like whey or a protein shake to kickstart recovery.
data.TIP_TEXT: Blend 1 scoop whey, 1 banana, 1 cup milk, and a handful of spinach for a complete post-workout recovery drink.

data.HEADLINE: 5 Hydration Myths Busted
data.BODY: You don't need 8 glasses a day. Your water needs depend on body weight, activity level, and climate. Clear urine isn't always better — pale yellow is the sweet spot.
data.TIP_TEXT: Weigh yourself before and after exercise. For every pound lost, drink 16-20 oz of water to rehydrate properly.

data.HEADLINE: Your Spring Fitness Nutrition Reset
data.SUBTITLE: Fuel your outdoor workouts with smarter eating habits this April
data.FEATURE1_DESC: Load up on asparagus, peas, and strawberries for peak nutrient density
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Eat your largest carb meal 2-3 hours before outdoor training sessions
data.FEATURE3_DESC: Add electrolytes when exercising outdoors for more than 45 minutes

data.HEADLINE: Does Meal Timing Really Matter?
data.BODY: Total daily intake matters most, but strategic timing can optimize performance and recovery. Spread protein across 4-5 meals for maximum muscle protein synthesis.
data.TIP_TEXT: Eat every 3-4 hours: breakfast within 1 hour of waking, pre-workout 2 hours before, post-workout within 1 hour, and a casein-rich snack before bed.

data.HEADLINE: Stop Fearing Fat: A Macro Guide
data.BODY: Dietary fat is essential for hormone production, brain function, and vitamin absorption. Aim for 25-35% of total calories from fat, prioritizing unsaturated sources.
data.TIP_TEXT: Replace butter with avocado oil for cooking, snack on almonds instead of chips, and add ground flaxseed to smoothies for omega-3s.

data.HEADLINE: Meal Prep: Chaos vs. System
data.AFTER_STAT3: 5 meals prepped every Sunday in 90 min

data.FOOD_NAME: Grilled Chicken Bowl with Quinoa & Roasted Veggies

data.HEADLINE: 5 High-Protein Breakfasts Under 400 Cal
data.BODY: Starting your day with 30g+ protein reduces cravings, stabilizes blood sugar, and supports lean muscle. These quick options take under 10 minutes.
data.STAT_LABEL: Protein target for your first meal
data.TIP_TEXT: 1 cup plain Greek yogurt (20g protein) + 1 scoop protein powder (25g) + berries + 1 tbsp chia seeds = 380 cal, 48g protein.

data.HEADLINE: The Forgotten Macro: Fiber
data.BODY: Most people eat only 15g of fiber daily — half the recommended amount. Fiber supports gut health, satiety, blood sugar control, and heart health.
data.TIP_TEXT: Add 1 cup of lentils (15g fiber), swap white rice for black beans, and eat fruit whole instead of juiced. Build up gradually to avoid bloating.

data.HEADLINE: Scan. Log. Learn. In Seconds.
data.SUBTITLE: NourishAI's camera scans your plate and logs every macro instantly
data.FEATURE1_DESC: AI identifies foods on your plate with 95% accuracy in under 2 seconds
data.FEATURE2_DESC: See calories, protein, carbs, fat, and micronutrients immediately
data.FEATURE3_DESC: Get real-time tips to balance your meal based on daily targets

data.HEADLINE: Simple Sugar vs. Complex Carbs
data.BODY: Not all carbs are created equal. Simple sugars spike blood glucose and crash energy, while complex carbs provide sustained fuel. Choose whole grains, legumes, and starchy vegetables.
data.STAT_LABEL: of daily calories should come from quality carbs
data.TIP_TEXT: White bread to sourdough. Sugary cereal to steel-cut oats. Candy to dates with almond butter. Soda to sparkling water with citrus.

data.HEADLINE: What to Eat Before You Train
data.BODY: The right pre-workout meal maximizes performance and prevents fatigue. Focus on easily digestible carbs with moderate protein 2-3 hours before, or a small snack 30-45 minutes before.
data.TIP_TEXT: A banana with 1 tbsp peanut butter, or a rice cake with honey and a pinch of salt. Keep fat and fiber low to avoid stomach issues.

data.HEADLINE: Your Weekly Nutrition Report Card
data.SUBTITLE: NourishAI analyzes 7 days of data to show you exactly where to improve
data.FEATURE1_DESC: See your macro consistency, calorie trends, and nutrient gaps at a glance
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Track your weekly averages against protein, calorie, and fiber targets
data.FEATURE3_DESC: AI-generated recommendations based on your actual eating patterns

data.HEADLINE: Snacking Smart: Fuel, Don't Fill
data.BODY: Strategic snacking bridges meals and prevents overeating. Pair protein with fiber for satiety — avoid snacking out of boredom. Plan your snacks like you plan your meals.
data.TIP_TEXT: 1) Cottage cheese + cucumber (20g protein, 120 cal). 2) Apple + 2 tbsp almond butter (8g protein, 250 cal). 3) Turkey roll-ups with hummus (18g protein, 180 cal).

data.HEADLINE: Grocery Shopping: Guessing vs. Guided
data.BEFORE_STAT2: 40% of fresh produce went to waste
data.BEFORE_STAT3: Pantry full but nothing to cook
data.AFTER_STAT3: Every item maps to a planned recipe

data.HEADLINE: Order Smarter at Any Restaurant
data.BODY: Eating out doesn't mean blowing your macros. Scan the menu with NourishAI before you order. Focus on grilled proteins, ask for dressings on the side, and skip the bread basket.
data.TIP_TEXT: Pick a protein first, add two veggie sides, request sauce on the side, and drink water before the meal. Box half the entree immediately if portions are large.

data.BEFORE_STAT3: No idea what was actually needed
data.AFTER_STAT3: Vitamin D, magnesium, and omega-3 — confirmed gaps

data.HEADLINE: Sleep Better by Eating Smarter
data.SUBTITLE: Your evening nutrition directly impacts sleep quality and next-day performance
data.FEATURE1_DESC: Complex carbs at dinner boost serotonin and melatonin production for deeper sleep
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Slow-digesting protein like cottage cheese supports overnight muscle recovery
data.FEATURE3_DESC: NourishAI flags caffeine intake after 2pm and tracks its impact on your sleep data
